rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the intended target component or implementation while preserving AADL architecture
Place assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes when referring to component ports
Place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections
Do not use subcomponent ports in a component type contract unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Write output dependency contracts that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Place assumptions and guarantees in the most appropriate scope for the referenced features
Avoid using subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are properly represented
Write output dependency contracts that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` for assumptions and guarantees
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for equations and `const name : type = value;` for constants
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless they are properly represented
Do not encode AADL property associations as AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as typed ports or constants
Do not write output dependency contracts that use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type annexes
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
